findings.summary: All three executed fixed-fee engagement letters state the fee but specify no payment terms: no invoicing schedule, retainer, installments, or net terms for a signed six-figure contract.
findings.evidence: d:0001: 'Our fee for the review is a fixed fee of {{fact}}, which covers the work described above from start through delivery. There are no hourly charges to track and no reconciliation at the end. Any out-of-pocket costs ... billed only as incurred, with documentation.' The letter spells out how trivial out-of-pockets are billed but never says when or how the ~$100k fee itself is invoiced or paid. Same in d:0005 ('billed only as incurred') and d:0008 ('nothing to reconcile at the close'). A CFO/VP Finance signing an EXECUTED fixed-fee agreement would expect a billing schedule.
